_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Federal batch: HI-02 Tokuda + IA delegation (Miller-Meeks IA-01, Nunn IA-03, Feenstra IA-04 lame duck) + IA-04 placeholder resolved to 2026 R nominee Chris McGowan. Evidence-only; roll calls from clerk.house.gov; candidacy currency verified against 2026-06-02 Iowa primary results.

profile.background: US Rep HI-02 since Jan 2023; HI state senator 2006-2018 (Ways & Means chair); House Armed Services + Agriculture committees.

jill-tokuda.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10. Consistent Democratic record on every marquee roll call located: Nay H.R. 26 Born-Alive (2023 roll 29), Nay H.R. 2 Secure the Border (2023 roll 209), No SAVE Act twice (2024 roll 345, 2025 roll 102), Nay Laken Riley Act (2025 roll 23), Nay H.R. 28 women's sports (2025 roll 12), No CBDC Anti-Surveillance Act (2024 roll 230), No H.R. 5 Parents Bill of Rights (2023 roll 161). Equality Act (H.R. 15, 119th) cosponsor. EMILY's List + Planned Parenthood Alliance Advocates HI endorsements accepted. Giffords PAC endorsed. Heritage Action 9%, SBA Pro-Life F, NRL 0%. No cross-party deviations found; bipartisan Yea on TikTok divestment (2024 roll 86) noted but not a conservative deviation. Genuine Red Hill military/contractor accountability advocacy credited (HASC). Seeking reelection 2026 (HI-02, D+12; GOP opponent Brenton Awa). AIPAC/foreign-lobby funding unverified - left null.

1.note: Voted Nay on H.R. 26 Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (2023 roll 29); SBA Pro-Life F, National Right to Life 0%, Planned Parenthood 100%.

4.note: Accepted and promotes EMILY's List endorsement (since 2006) and Planned Parenthood Alliance Advocates HI endorsement.

2.note: Voted Nay on passage of H.R. 28 Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (2025 roll 12).

4.note: Cosponsor of H.R. 15 Equality Act (119th Congress).

1.note: Voted No on H.R. 5 Parents Bill of Rights Act (2023 roll 161), which included parental notification/consent provisions for school gender-related changes.

0.note: Voted No on H.R. 5403 CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act (2024 roll 230).

1.note: Voted No on the SAVE Act twice: H.R. 8281 (2024 roll 345) and H.R. 22 (2025 roll 102).

0.note: Voted Nay on H.R. 2 Secure the Border Act (2023 roll 209), whose core provisions resume border-wall construction.

1.note: Voted Nay on S. 5 Laken Riley Act (2025 roll 23) mandating detention/removal of criminal illegal aliens; also Nay on H.R. 8146 Police Our Border Act (2024 roll 215).

3.note: H.R. 2 Nay also covers its mandatory nationwide E-Verify title.

1.note: Giffords PAC endorsement - formal gun-control endorsement is hard contrary evidence on opposing red-flag laws/AWBs.

4.note: Credit where earned: HASC member with sustained public Navy/contractor accountability advocacy on Red Hill fuel-spill failures (own statements demanding accountability).

profile.background: US Rep IA-01 since 2021; ophthalmologist (MD); 24-year Army veteran (retired Lt. Col.); former director, Iowa Dept. of Public Health. Heritage Action lifetime 75%.

mariannette-miller-meeks.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10. Mostly conservative roll-call record (Yea: H.R. 26 Born-Alive 2023-29, H.R. 2 border 2023-209, SAVE Act 2025-102, H.R. 28 women's sports 2025-12, anti-CBDC H.R. 1919 2025-201, ESG CRA H.J.Res. 30, H.R. 5 Parents Bill of Rights 2023-161, H.R. 497 end CMS vaccine mandate 2023-98; Nay Equality Act 2021-39) with documented CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS: (1) voted YEA on the Respect for Marriage Act twice (2022 rolls 373 + 513), drawing Iowa county-GOP censure efforts; (2) pro-IVF - withdrew her Life at Conception Act cosponsorship over its personhood/IVF implications and introduced an IVF tax-credit bill; (3) Yea on $60B Ukraine supplemental (2024 roll 151), though Nay on the 2026 Ukraine Support Act (2026 roll 207); (4) Nay on 2002-AUMF repeal (2021 roll 172); (5) chairs the Conservative Climate Caucus. SBA Pro-Life A+ and endorsed. CANDIDACY: won the 2026-06-02 IA-01 GOP primary over Pautsch and Hill; faces D Christina Bohannan rematch in November. NRA grade current cycle unverified (A in 2014) - left null. AIPAC totals unverified - left null.

0.note: Cosponsored the Life at Conception Act, then removed herself as cosponsor over its personhood-from-conception/IVF implications - a documented retreat from the personhood position.

2.note: Actively champions IVF as practiced - introduced a $30k IVF tax-credit bill; contrary to opposing IVF embryo discard.

4.note: SBA Pro-Life America A+ rating and endorsement; no evidence of Planned Parenthood/NARAL/EMILY's List funding found.

0.note: Voted Yea on the Respect for Marriage Act both times (July 2022 roll 373, Dec 2022 final roll 513), codifying federal recognition of same-sex marriage.

1.note: RFMA Yea (final passage 2022 roll 513); Iowa county GOP parties sought a primary challenger over the vote.

2.note: Voted Yea on H.R. 28 Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (2025 roll 12).

4.note: Voted Nay on the Equality Act (2021 roll 39).

0.note: Cosponsor of the Educational Choice for Children Act (federal school-choice tax credit).

2.note: Voted Aye on H.R. 5 Parents Bill of Rights Act (2023 roll 161).

0.note: Voted Yea on the Anti-CBDC Surveillance State Act, H.R. 1919 (2025 roll 201).

4.note: Voted Yea on H.J.Res. 30 CRA disapproving the DOL ESG-investing rule.

1.note: Voted Yea on the SAVE Act, H.R. 22 (2025 roll 102) - photo ID with citizenship verification for federal voter registration.

0.note: Voted Yea on H.R. 2 Secure the Border Act (2023 roll 209), resuming border-wall construction.

3.note: H.R. 2 Yea includes its mandatory nationwide E-Verify title.

4.note: Cosponsored bill barring PRC purchases of US farmland (farmland prong documented; birthright-citizenship prong: no evidence found).
Joined formal letter opposing the ATF pistol-brace rule as agency overreach.

1.note: Voted Nay on H.R. 256 repealing the 2002 Iraq AUMF (2021 roll 172).

0.note: Voted Aye on H.R. 497 Freedom for Health Care Workers Act ending the CMS vaccine mandate (2023 roll 98) and Yea on H.J.Res. 7 ending the COVID national emergency.

profile.religion: Roman Catholic (per Presidential Prayer Team profile)
profile.background: US Rep IA-03 since 2023; USAF intelligence officer (still serving in Reserve); Iowa House 2015-19, Iowa Senate 2019-23. Heritage Action lifetime 57%.

zach-nunn.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10. Conservative on most marquee roll calls (Yea: H.R. 26 Born-Alive 2023-29, H.R. 2 border 2023-209, SAVE Act 2025-102, H.R. 28 women's sports 2025-12, CBDC Anti-Surveillance H.R. 5403 2024-228, H.J.Res. 44 pistol-brace CRA 2023-252, Laken Riley S.5 2025-23; voted for Iowa constitutional carry HF 756 in 2021 as state senator) with documented DEVIATIONS: (1) opposes a federal abortion ban - states-should-decide position with rape/incest/life exceptions (own 2024 campaign statements); (2) explicitly champions IVF/fertility-treatment access; (3) Heritage Action 57%, low for an Iowa R; (4) Yea on $61B Ukraine supplemental (2024 roll 151); (5) Not Voting on H.R. 5 Parents Bill of Rights (2023 roll 161) and FIT21 (absences, not evidence). Sponsored the bipartisan Child Online Safety Modernization Act and co-led farmland foreign-investment oversight bill. CANDIDACY: floated a 2026 governor run, dropped it July 2025 after a call from Trump; seeking IA-03 reelection 2026 (expected D opponent Sarah Trone Garriott). Current NRA grade and AIPAC totals unverified - left null.

1.note: Own campaign statement: opposes a federal abortion ban, says states should decide, supports rape/incest/life exceptions - documented contrary to abolition. (Did vote Yea on H.R. 26 Born-Alive, 2023 roll 29.)

2.note: Explicitly champions IVF/fertility-treatment access (own statement) - contrary to opposing IVF embryo discard.
Voted Yea on H.R. 28 Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (2025 roll 12); own statement 'For Our Daughters.'

3.note: Sponsored the Child Online Safety Modernization Act (CyberTipline/NCMEC modernization targeting online child sexual exploitation). Note: Not Voting on H.R. 5 Parents Bill of Rights (2023 roll 161) - absence, not scored.

0.note: Voted Aye on the CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act, H.R. 5403 (2024 roll 228).

1.note: Voted Yea on the SAVE Act, H.R. 22 (2025 roll 102).

0.note: Voted Yea on H.R. 2 Secure the Border Act (2023 roll 209), resuming border-wall construction.

3.note: H.R. 2 Yea includes its mandatory nationwide E-Verify title.

4.note: Co-led the Protecting U.S. Farmland and Sensitive Sites from Foreign Adversaries Act (farmland prong documented; birthright prong: no evidence found).

0.note: Iowa constitutional-carry HF 756 (2021) passed the Iowa Senate with all 31 Republicans voting yes; Nunn was an R senator - recorded party-unanimous vote.

4.note: Voted Yea on H.J.Res. 44 CRA disapproving the ATF pistol-brace rule (2023 roll 252).

set.office: US Representative IA-04 (lame duck - lost 2026 IA Governor R primary; not seeking House reelection)

profile.religion: Christian (Dutch Reformed milieu, Dordt College; per VoteSmart bio)
profile.background: US Rep IA-04 since 2021 (defeated Steve King in 2020 primary); Iowa Senate 2009-2021 (Ways & Means chair); former Dordt Univ. business professor. Heritage Action lifetime 81%.
profile.confidence_note: Candidacy currency web-verified 2026-06-10: lost the 2026-06-02 IA Governor GOP primary to Zach Lahn (~37.8-37.0) despite Trump endorsement; did not seek House reelection, so he holds no spot on any Nov 2026 ballot. IA-04 R nominee is Chris McGowan.

randy-feenstra.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10. Strong conservative record: cosponsored Life at Conception Act H.R. 431; Iowa Senate sponsor of the 2018 fetal-heartbeat ban SF 359; Yea H.R. 26 Born-Alive (2023 roll 29); NAY on the Respect for Marriage Act (2022 roll 513) citing religious-liberty threats to churches/nonprofits - the conservative side where IA colleagues Hinson and Miller-Meeks voted Yea; Yea H.R. 28 (2025 roll 12), H.R. 5 Parents Bill of Rights (2023 roll 161), SAVE Act (2025 roll 102), H.R. 2 border (2023 roll 209), CBDC Anti-Surveillance (2024 roll 230), H.J.Res. 44 pistol-brace CRA (2023 roll 252). SBA Pro-Life A+; NRA A. Leads bills restricting Chinese/adversary farmland purchases. DEVIATIONS: Yea $60.8B Ukraine supplemental (2024 roll 151); Heritage 118th-session score dipped to 64%. House Ag ethanol/Farm Bill champion (subsidy advocacy - Big Ag consolidation cell left null). CANDIDACY: launched for IA Governor Oct 2025; LOST the 2026-06-02 GOP primary to Zach Lahn; lame-duck House term ends Jan 2027; not on any November 2026 ballot.

0.note: Cosponsored the Life at Conception Act, H.R. 431 (118th) - personhood from conception.

1.note: Life at Conception Act cosponsorship (abolition-level personhood legislation); Iowa Senate sponsor of the 2018 fetal-heartbeat ban SF 359; Yea H.R. 26 Born-Alive (2023 roll 29).

4.note: SBA Pro-Life America A+ rating; no evidence of abortion-industry funding found.

1.note: Voted Nay on the Respect for Marriage Act final passage (2022 roll 513), publicly explaining his opposition.

2.note: Voted Yea on H.R. 28 Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (2025 roll 12).
Voted Aye on H.R. 5 Parents Bill of Rights Act (2023 roll 161).

1.note: Own statement: opposed RFMA because it leaves churches and faith-based nonprofits 'open to frivolous lawsuits' - advocacy for institutional religious-conscience protection.

0.note: Voted Aye on H.R. 5403 CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act (2024 roll 230).

1.note: Voted Yea on the SAVE Act, H.R. 22 (2025 roll 102).

0.note: Voted Yea on H.R. 2 Secure the Border Act (2023 roll 209).

3.note: H.R. 2 Yea includes its mandatory nationwide E-Verify title.

4.note: Leads legislation restricting Chinese/foreign-adversary purchases of US farmland (farmland prong documented; birthright prong: no evidence found).
Voted Yea on H.J.Res. 44 CRA disapproving the ATF pistol-brace rule (2023 roll 252).

set.name: Chris McGowan
set.office: U.S. Representative IA-04 (2026 R nominee - open seat, Feenstra seat)

profile.religion: Roman Catholic (Bishop Heelan Catholic HS; St. John's University; Ave Maria School of Law JD)
profile.background: President, Siouxland Chamber of Commerce; Iowa Air National Guard veteran; lawyer; fifth-generation NW Iowa. Won the 2026 IA-04 GOP primary unopposed after Trump's endorsement cleared the field.
profile.confidence_note: First-time candidate, no voting record - scored only from his own cited campaign statements per the social-media protocol; thin denominator (one scored cell), read the headline score accordingly. Roster/candidacy web-verified 2026-06-10 against 2026-06-02 primary results.

ia-04-r-placeholder.notes_set: Placeholder resolved 2026-06-10: Chris McGowan is the 2026 IA-04 Republican nominee (only R on the primary ballot after Matt Windschitl suspended Feb 2026 and endorsed him; Rhodes, Schlaefer, Jensen also exited). Faces Democrat Dave Dawson (former state rep, won D primary 39.1%) in November; IA-04 is Iowa's most Republican district. Endorsements: Trump, Vance, Speaker Johnson, Scalise, Emmer, Jordan, Ernst, Branstad, Windschitl (broad-spectrum, not single-issue - not scored). Campaign vision page commits to 'defending life from conception to natural death' (scored). Border: pledges to fight fentanyl/trafficking and an 'impenetrable' border - no specific barrier/military-presence commitment, left null. Marriage/trans, school choice, guns, election integrity, CBDC: no clear citable positions found - left null per evidence discipline. No SBA or NRA endorsement found as of June 2026.

0.note: Campaign vision page commits to 'defending life from conception to natural death' - clear self-stated position.
